intro: Off-hours: practices that feed the eye and thinking about cities and forms.

items.title: Architectural Photography
items.description: Documenting architecture during travels — Lisbon, Brasília, Belo Horizonte, Niterói. Hundreds of photos catalogued by city and typology, from art deco to brut concrete.

items.title: Urban Cycling
items.description: Cycling is the best way to read the urban fabric. Each ride reveals a different city.

items.title: Books on Cities
items.description: Jane Jacobs, Saskia Sassen, Milton Santos. Trying to understand why some cities sing and others only function.

items.title: Woodworking
items.description: Building small is understanding scale. A table project teaches more about detail than three years of theory.

items.title: Typology Travel
items.description: I visit cities not for monuments, but to see how people live. Hanoi, Lisbon, Tokyo, Mexico City.

items.title: Observational Drawing
items.description: Black notebook, pen. Before measuring a space, draw it — the hand catches things the ruler doesn't.
